title: rubus schleicheri var. samuelis
intro: Rubus schleicheri var. samuelis, also known as the black raspberry, is a type of bramble that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). It is a perennial plant with edible fruits, rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and cancer, due to its high content of anthocyanins and ellagic acid. In herbal preparations, black raspberry is often used as a supplement or tincture, and it can be consumed as a juice, tea, or powder to reap its health benefits.
